_comment: Drug-drug interaction rules for common medications in the Nigerian National Formulary. Severity: contraindicated | major | moderate | minor.

interactions.description: Concurrent use significantly increases risk of bleeding. Avoid combination unless benefit clearly outweighs risk.
interactions.action: Monitor closely for signs of bleeding. Consider alternative antiplatelet agent.

interactions.description: Risk of contrast-induced nephropathy leading to metformin-associated lactic acidosis.
interactions.action: Hold metformin 48 hours before and after iodinated contrast procedures. Check renal function before resuming.

interactions.description: Antacids containing aluminium/magnesium chelate fluoroquinolones, reducing absorption by up to 90%.
interactions.action: Administer ciprofloxacin 2 hours before or 6 hours after antacid.

interactions.description: ACE inhibitors reduce renal potassium excretion; concurrent potassium supplementation can cause hyperkalaemia.
interactions.action: Monitor serum potassium regularly. Avoid concurrent use unless serum K < 3.5 mEq/L.

interactions.description: Combined use increases risk of serotonin syndrome and lowers seizure threshold.
interactions.action: Avoid combination. If necessary, use lowest effective tramadol dose and monitor for serotonin syndrome symptoms.

interactions.description: Disulfiram-like reaction with severe nausea, vomiting, flushing, and hypotension.
interactions.action: Contraindicated. Advise patient to avoid alcohol during treatment and for 48 hours after the last dose.

interactions.description: Antibiotics can disrupt gut flora and reduce Vitamin K synthesis, potentially increasing INR.
interactions.action: Monitor INR more frequently during antibiotic course and for 1 week after completion.

interactions.description: Amiodarone inhibits P-glycoprotein and CYP3A4, significantly increasing digoxin plasma levels.
interactions.action: Reduce digoxin dose by 30–50% when initiating amiodarone. Monitor digoxin levels and ECG.

interactions.description: Clarithromycin inhibits CYP3A4, massively increasing simvastatin exposure and risk of myopathy/rhabdomyolysis.
interactions.action: Contraindicated. Temporarily suspend simvastatin for the duration of clarithromycin treatment.

interactions.description: NSAIDs reduce the antihypertensive effect of ACE inhibitors and increase risk of acute kidney injury.
interactions.action: Avoid combination when possible. If necessary, monitor renal function and blood pressure.
